You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: b139c14d).

## Issue to Resolve

Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE - https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#non-constant_names
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val SOME_MAP: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Create object, with mutable collection property. 

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.50.0

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

You're right. Can you provide a little more context where this variable is declared? Is it at top level, or in a companion object or class?
I have edited the issue, so you can see where the property is declared. 

In my case it is an object's property. I did not check for other cases as top level or companion object's variables (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming should not be applied to class properties according to https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22â†’
 23â†’/**
 24â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26â†’ */
 27â†’public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28â†’    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29â†’    Rule.Experimental {
 30â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31â†’        node: ASTNode,
 32â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34â†’    ) {
 35â†’        node
 36â†’            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37â†’            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38â†’    }
 39â†’
 40â†’    private fun visitProperty(
 41â†’        property: ASTNode,
 42â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43â†’    ) {
 44â†’        property
 45â†’            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46â†’            ?.let { identifier ->
 47â†’                when {
 48â†’                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49â†’                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50â†’                    }
 51â†’                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52â†’                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53â†’                    }
 54â†’                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55â†’                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56â†’                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57â†’                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58â†’                    }
 59â†’                    else -> {
 60â†’                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61â†’                    }
 62â†’                }
 63â†’            }
 64â†’    }
 65â†’
 66â†’    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67â†’        identifier: ASTNode,
 68â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69â†’    ) {
 70â†’        identifier
 71â†’            .text
 72â†’            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73â†’            ?.let {
 74â†’                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75â†’            }
 76â†’    }
 77â†’
 78â†’    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79â†’        identifier: ASTNode,
 80â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81â†’    ) {
 82â†’        identifier
 83â†’            .text
 84â†’            .takeUnless {
 85â†’                // Allow
 86â†’                // object Foo {
 87â†’                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 88â†’                // }
 89â†’                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 90â†’            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 91â†’            ?.let {
 92â†’                emit(
 93â†’                    identifier.startOffset,
 94â†’                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 95â†’                    false,
 96â†’                )
 97â†’            }
 98â†’    }
 99â†’
100â†’    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
101â†’        identifier: ASTNode,
102â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103â†’    ) {
104â†’        identifier
105â†’            .text
106â†’            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
107â†’            ?.let {
108â†’                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
109â†’            }
110â†’    }
111â†’
112â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
113â†’
114â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
115â†’
116â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
117â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
118â†’            ?.children()
119â†’            .orEmpty()
120â†’            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
121â†’
122â†’    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
123â†’
124â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
125â†’
126â†’    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
127â†’        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
128â†’            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
129â†’            containsValKeyword() &&
130â†’            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
131â†’
132â†’    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
133â†’        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
134â†’            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
135â†’            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
136â†’            ?.let { identifier ->
137â†’                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
138â†’            }
139â†’            ?: false
140â†’
141â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
142â†’        treeParent
143â†’            .children()
144â†’            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
145â†’            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
146â†’            .any { it.text == identifier }
147â†’
148â†’    private companion object {
149â†’        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150â†’        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
151â†’        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
152â†’        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
153â†’    }
154â†’}
155â†’
156â†’public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
157â†’

1â†’!!! important
   2â†’    Up and until Ktlint version `0.47`, experimental were located in a separate `experimental` rule set. As of Ktlint version `0.48`, each rule set can optionally define experimental rules.
   3â†’
   4â†’All experimental rules described below are part of the `standard` rule set of Ktlint. To enable all experimental rules (from all rule sets), set `editorconfig` property below:
   5â†’```ini
   6â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
   7â†’ktlint_experimental=enabled
   8â†’```
   9â†’Also see [enable/disable specific rules](../configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules).
  10â†’
  11â†’## Binary expression wrapping
  12â†’
  13â†’Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
  14â†’
  15â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  16â†’
  17â†’    ```kotlin
  18â†’    fun foo() {
  19â†’        // Assume that the last allowed character is
  20â†’        // at the X character on the right                       X
  21â†’        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
  22â†’            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
  23â†’                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
  24â†’            // do something
  25â†’        }
  26â†’    }
  27â†’    ```
  28â†’
  29â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  30â†’
  31â†’    ```kotlin
  32â†’    fun foo() {
  33â†’        // Assume that the last allowed character is
  34â†’        // at the X character on the right                       X
  35â†’        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
  36â†’            // do something
  37â†’        }
  38â†’    }
  39â†’    ```
  40â†’
  41â†’Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
  42â†’
  43â†’## Blank line before declarations
  44â†’
  45â†’Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.
  46â†’
  47â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  48â†’
  49â†’    ```kotlin
  50â†’    const val foo1 = "foo1"
  51â†’
  52â†’    class FooBar {
  53â†’        val foo2 = "foo2"
  54â†’        val foo3 = "foo3"
  55â†’
  56â†’        fun bar1() {
  57â†’           val foo4 = "foo4"
  58â†’           val foo5 = "foo5"
  59â†’        }
  60â†’
  61â†’        fun bar2() = "bar"
  62â†’
  63â†’        val foo6 = "foo3"
  64â†’        val foo7 = "foo4"
  65â†’
  66â†’        enum class Foo {}
  67â†’    }
  68â†’    ```
  69â†’
  70â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  71â†’
  72â†’    ```kotlin
  73â†’    const val foo1 = "foo1"
  74â†’    class FooBar {
  75â†’        val foo2 = "foo2"
  76â†’        val foo3 = "foo3"
  77â†’        fun bar1() {
  78â†’           val foo4 = "foo4"
  79â†’           val foo5 = "foo5"
  80â†’        }
  81â†’        fun bar2() = "bar"
  82â†’        val foo6 = "foo3"
  83â†’        val foo7 = "foo4"
  84â†’        enum class Foo {}
  85â†’    }
  86â†’    ```
  87â†’
  88â†’Rule id: `blank-line-before-declaration` (`standard` rule set)
  89â†’
  90â†’!!! Note
  91â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
  92â†’
  93â†’## Class signature
  94â†’
  95â†’Rewrites the class signature to a consistent format respecting the `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` if set. In the `ktlint_official` code style all class parameters are wrapped by default. Set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_class_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters` to a value greater than 1 to allow class with a few parameters to be placed on a single line.
  96â†’The other code styles allow an infinite amount of parameters on the same line (as long as the `max_line_length` is not exceeded) unless `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_class_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters` is set explicitly.
  97â†’
  98â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint (ktlint_official)"

 317â†’## Disallow consecutive comments
 318â†’
 319â†’Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
 320â†’- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
 321â†’- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
 322â†’- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
 323â†’
 324â†’Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
 325â†’
 326â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 327â†’
 328â†’    ```kotlin
 329â†’    // An EOL comment
 330â†’    // may be followed by another EOL comment
 331â†’    val foo = "foo"
 332â†’
 333â†’    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
 334â†’
 335â†’    /*
 336â†’     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
 337â†’     */
 338â†’
 339â†’    /**
 340â†’      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
 341â†’      */
 342â†’    fun bar() = "bar"
 343â†’    ```
 344â†’
 345â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 346â†’
 347â†’    ```kotlin
 348â†’    /*
 349â†’     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
 350â†’     */
 351â†’    /*
 352â†’     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
 353â†’     */
 354â†’    val bar = "bar" 
 355â†’
 356â†’    /**
 357â†’      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
 358â†’      */
 359â†’
 360â†’    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
 361â†’    ```
 362â†’
 363â†’Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
 364â†’
 365â†’!!! Note
 366â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 367â†’
 368â†’## Function literal
 369â†’
 370â†’Enforces the parameters of a function literal and the arrow to be written on the same line as the opening brace if the maximum line length is not exceeded. In case the parameters are wrapped to multiple lines then this is respected.
 371â†’
 372â†’If the function literal contains multiple parameter and at least one parameter other than the first parameter starts on a new line than all parameters and the arrow are wrapped to separate lines.
 373â†’
 374â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 375â†’
 376â†’    ```kotlin
 377â†’            val foobar1 = { foo + bar }
 378â†’            val foobar2 =
 379â†’                {
 380â†’                    foo + bar
 381â†’                }
 382â†’            val foobar3 =
 383â†’                { foo: Foo ->
 384â†’                    foo.repeat(2)
 385â†’                }
 386â†’            val foobar4 =
 387â†’                { foo: Foo, bar: Bar ->
 388â†’                    foo + bar
 389â†’                }
 390â†’            val foobar5 = { foo: Foo, bar: Bar -> foo + bar }
 391â†’            val foobar6 =
 392â†’                {
 393â†’                        foo: Foo,
 394â†’                        bar: Bar
 395â†’                    ->
 396â†’                    foo + bar
 397â†’                }
 398â†’
 399â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 400â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 401â†’    val foobar7 =
 402â†’        barrrrrrrrrrrrrr {
 403â†’                fooooooooooooooo: Foo
 404â†’            ->
 405â†’            foo.repeat(2)
 406â†’        }
 407â†’    ```
 408â†’
 409â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 410â†’
 411â†’    ```kotlin
 412â†’    val foobar3 =
 413â†’        {
 414â†’            foo: Foo ->
 415â†’            foo.repeat(2)
 416â†’        }
 417â†’    val foobar6 =
 418â†’        { foo: Foo,
 419â†’          bar: Bar ->
 420â†’            foo + bar
 421â†’        }
 422â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 423â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 424â†’    val foobar7 =
 425â†’        barrrrrrrrrrrrrr { fooooooooooooooo: Foo ->
 426â†’            foo.repeat(2)
 427â†’        }
 428â†’    ```
 429â†’
 430â†’Rule id: `function-literal` (`standard` rule set)
 431â†’
 432â†’!!! Note
 433â†’This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.

 435â†’## Function signature
 436â†’
 437â†’Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
 438â†’
 439â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 440â†’
 441â†’    ```kotlin
 442â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 443â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 444â†’    fun foooooooo(
 445â†’        a: Any,
 446â†’        b: Any,
 447â†’        c: Any
 448â†’    ): String {
 449â†’        // body
 450â†’    }
 451â†’
 452â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 453â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 454â†’    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
 455â†’        // body
 456â†’    }
 457â†’
 458â†’    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
 459â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 460â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 461â†’    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 462â†’        .uppercase()
 463â†’
 464â†’    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
 465â†’    // or 'always'.
 466â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 467â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 468â†’    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
 469â†’        "some-result"
 470â†’            .uppercase()
 471â†’    ```
 472â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 473â†’
 474â†’    ```kotlin

 476â†’    // at the X character on the right           X

 478â†’        // body
 479â†’    }
 480â†’
 481â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 482â†’    // at the X character on the right           X
 483â†’    fun bar(
 484â†’        a: Any,
 485â†’        b: Any,
 486â†’        c: Any
 487â†’    ): String {
 488â†’        // body
 489â†’    }
 490â†’
 491â†’    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
 492â†’    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 493â†’    // at the X character on the right           X

 497â†’

 508â†’## If else bracing
 509â†’
 510â†’If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
 511â†’
 512â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 513â†’
 514â†’    ```kotlin
 515â†’    fun foo(value: int) {
 516â†’        if (value > 0) {
 517â†’            doSomething()
 518â†’        } else if (value < 0) {
 519â†’            doSomethingElse()
 520â†’        } else {
 521â†’            doSomethingElse2()
 522â†’        }
 523â†’    }
 524â†’    ```
 525â†’
 526â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 527â†’
 528â†’    ```kotlin
 529â†’    fun foo(value: int) {
 530â†’        if (value > 0)
 531â†’            doSomething()
 532â†’        else if (value < 0) {
 533â†’            doSomethingElse()
 534â†’        } else
 535â†’            doSomethingElse2()
 536â†’    }
 537â†’    ```
 538â†’
 539â†’Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
 540â†’
 541â†’!!! Note
 542â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 543â†’
 544â†’## If else wrapping
 545â†’
 546â†’A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
 547â†’
 548â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 549â†’
 550â†’    ```kotlin
 551â†’    fun foobar() {
 552â†’        if (true) foo()
 553â†’        if (true) foo() else bar()
 554â†’    }
 555â†’    ```
 556â†’
 557â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 558â†’
 559â†’    ```kotlin
 560â†’    fun foobar() {
 561â†’        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
 562â†’        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
 563â†’        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
 564â†’        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
 565â†’    }
 566â†’    ```
 567â†’
 568â†’Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 569â†’
 570â†’!!! Note
 571â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.

 573â†’## Naming
 574â†’
 575â†’### Function naming
 576â†’
 577â†’Enforce naming of function. 
 578â†’
 579â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 580â†’
 581â†’    ```kotlin
 582â†’    fun foo() {}
 583â†’    fun fooBar() {}
 584â†’    ```
 585â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
 586â†’
 587â†’    ```kotlin
 588â†’    @Test
 589â†’    fun `Some name`() {}
 590â†’
 591â†’    @Test
 592â†’    fun do_something() {}
 593â†’    ```
 594â†’
 595â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 596â†’
 597â†’    ```kotlin

 599â†’    fun Foo_Bar() {}
 600â†’    fun `Some name`() {}
 601â†’    fun do_something() {}
 602â†’    ```
 603â†’
 604â†’!!! note
 605â†’    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.

 607â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.
 608â†’
 609â†’Rule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 610â†’
 611â†’### Package naming
 612â†’
 613â†’Enforce naming of package.
 614â†’
 615â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PackageName`.
 616â†’
 617â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 618â†’
 619â†’    ```kotlin

 621â†’    package foo.foo
 622â†’    package foo_bar
 623â†’    package foo.foo_bar
 624â†’    ```
 625â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 626â†’

 631â†’    package foo.`foo bar`
 632â†’    ```
 633â†’
 634â†’Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 635â†’
 636â†’### Property naming
 637â†’
 638â†’Enforce naming of property.
 639â†’
 640â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 641â†’
 642â†’    ```kotlin
 643â†’    val FOO_1 = Foo()
 644â†’    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
 645â†’
 646â†’    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
 647â†’
 648â†’    class Bar {
 649â†’        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
 650â†’        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
 651â†’
 652â†’        val foo2 = "foo"
 653â†’        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
 654â†’
 655â†’        // Backing property
 656â†’        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
 657â†’        val elementList: List<Element>
 658â†’            get() = _elementList
 659â†’    }
 660â†’    ```
 661â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 662â†’
 663â†’    ```kotlin
 664â†’    val Foo1 = Foo()
 665â†’    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
 666â†’
 667â†’    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
 668â†’
 669â†’    class Bar {
 670â†’        const val foo2 = "foo"
 671â†’        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
 672â†’
 673â†’        val FOO2 = "foo"
 674â†’        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
 675â†’
 676â†’        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
 677â†’        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
 678â†’
 679â†’        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
 680â†’        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
 681â†’        val elementList2: List<Element>
 682â†’            get() = _elementList2
 683â†’    }
 684â†’    ```
 685â†’
 686â†’!!! note
 687â†’    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
 688â†’
 689â†’This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
 690â†’
 691â†’Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)

 693â†’## No empty file
 694â†’
 695â†’A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.

 915â†’        return """

 917â†’            line2
 918â†’            """.trimIndent()
 919â†’    }
 920â†’    ```

content.text:
Implemented a targeted fix in `PropertyNamingRule` so that **mutable collection** properties are **not** treated as â€œconst-likeâ€‌ (and therefore no longer required to use `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`) when declared as a top-level `val` or as a `val` inside an object / companion object / object expression.

### What changed
- `PropertyNamingRule` now treats a top-level/object `val` as â€œconst-likeâ€‌ **only if it is not a mutable collection**.
- Mutable collections are detected via:
  - explicit type (e.g. `MutableMap`, `MutableList`, arrays, etc.), and
  - common mutable collection initializers/constructors (e.g. `mutableMapOf`, `mutableListOf`, `HashMap`, `ArrayList`, â€¦).

### Tests
- Added a new test file:
  - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRuleMutableCollectionTest.kt`

### Docs
- Updated the â€œProperty namingâ€‌ note in `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md` to reflect the mutable-collection exception.

 270â†’## Discouraged comment location
 271â†’
 272â†’Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 273â†’
 274â†’!!! note
 275â†’    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier.

 282â†’    fun foo() {
 283â†’        if (true)
 284â†’            // some comment
 285â†’            bar()
 286â†’    }
 287â†’    ```

 317â†’## Disallow consecutive comments
 318â†’
 319â†’Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
 320â†’- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
 321â†’- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
 322â†’- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
 323â†’
 324â†’Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
 325â†’
 326â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 327â†’
 328â†’    ```kotlin
 329â†’    // An EOL comment
 330â†’    // may be followed by another EOL comment
 331â†’    val foo = "foo"
 332â†’
 333â†’    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
 334â†’
 335â†’    /*
 336â†’     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
 337â†’     */
 338â†’
 339â†’    /**
 340â†’      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
 341â†’      */
 342â†’    fun bar() = "bar"
 343â†’    ```
 344â†’
 345â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 346â†’
 347â†’    ```kotlin
 348â†’    /*
 349â†’     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
 350â†’     */
 351â†’    /*
 352â†’     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
 353â†’     */
 354â†’    val bar = "bar" 
 355â†’
 356â†’    /**
 357â†’      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
 358â†’      */
 359â†’
 360â†’    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
 361â†’    ```
 362â†’
 363â†’Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
 364â†’
 365â†’!!! Note
 366â†’    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.

 368â†’## Function literal
 369â†’
 370â†’Enforces the parameters of a function literal and the arrow to be written on the same line as the opening brace if the maximum line length is not exceeded. In case the parameters are wrapped to multiple lines then this is respected.
 371â†’
 372â†’If the function literal contains multiple parameter and at least one parameter other than the first parameter starts on a new line than all parameters and the arrow are wrapped to separate lines.

 435â†’## Function signature
 436â†’
 437â†’Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)

 520â†’        } else {

 523â†’    }
 524â†’    ```

 544â†’## If else wrapping
 545â†’
 546â†’A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.

 553â†’        if (true) foo() else bar()
 554â†’    }
 555â†’    ```
 556â†’
 557â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 558â†’

 561â†’        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
 562â†’        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
 563â†’        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
 564â†’        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
 565â†’    }
 566â†’    ```
 567â†’

 693â†’## No empty file
